DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS(SET ONE) IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE DATE OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. JUDGE PRO TEM: NAOMI GRAY; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DORIAN LISBONA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SETS ONE THROUGH EIGHT) IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT EFRAIM SHOUA'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,887.50 IS GRANTED. THE SANCTIONS MUST BE PAID BY MR. LISBONA NO LATER THAN JANUARY 31, 2018. THE COURT ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATION AND REASONS FOR THE RECOMMENDATION IN THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM NOAH LEBOWITZ. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-25-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Continued To May-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CPF-18-516073 WITH CASE CGC-17-559401
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Alvin H. Lee of the law offices of Alvin H. Lee (925-210-1300) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Sets One Through Eight)
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DORIAN LISBONA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Sets One Through Eight) is DENIED in its entirety. Defendant Efraim Shoua's request for sanctions in the amount of $1,887.50 is granted. The sanctions must be paid by Mr. Lisbona no later than January 31, 2018. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasons for the recommendation in the report of Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz. In addition, the court determines that the form interrogatories are so confusing that the service of them and filing a motion to compel responses as to them lacks substantial justification.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
